select_when: A starting point, example, or framing device can help make a complex space more navigable without pretending it is final truth.
Leaders need an initial reference point for scope, expectation, or comparison while staying alert to later correction.
The team benefits from provisional orientation before deeper evidence arrives.
New information needs to be attached to an existing schema so attention and working memory can hold the problem long enough to think clearly.
avoid_when: The first number, frame, or analogy is becoming non-negotiable and later evidence is only being interpreted around it.
A familiar frame is being applied automatically to a genuinely novel problem, turning anchoring into substitution bias.
input_type: initial frame or reference point problem

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on deliberate orientation and reference-point setting while preserving the strong anti-entrenchment warnings.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style persuasion overlays because the raw source is stronger on focus, learning scaffolds, and correction discipline.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The output label is intentionally provisional because the source is clear that good anchoring must remain corrigible.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on useful starting frames, reference points, and schema-linked learning support.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source warnings on disconfirming-evidence blindness and substitution bias.

input_type.note: The source activates on first frames, expectations, and comparison points, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source clearly describes orienting reference points; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact that still preserves provisionality.

reasoning_types.note: Anchoring clearly works through analogical framing and reflective management of attention, but the reasoning taxonomy labels are reviewed normalization.
